code_change_summary.mori_config: Added standard_ep_compact_role to EpDispatchCombineConfig packing, pybind, and Python wrapper.
code_change_summary.allocation_change: Compact-only MORI handles allocate the large dispatchOut slab only for role 0/1 and the large combineInp slab only for role 0/2; unused role slabs are one byte.
code_change_summary.launch_guard: Native compact dispatch rejects combine-only handles, and native compact combine rejects dispatch-only handles.
code_change_summary.torchtitan_change: TorchTitan passes dispatch/combine/backward-dispatch/backward-combine stage roles when creating native compact MORI handles.
code_change_summary.gc_policy: Native compact handle cache release does not call Python gc.collect() by default; it is opt-in via TORCHTITAN_STANDARD_EP_MORI_NATIVE_COMPACT_GC_ON_RELEASE for memory-pressure diagnostics.
code_change_summary.perf_intent: Handle creation/lifetime only; no added row materialization, route masks, token collectives, per-row Python work, or per-token refcounting.

result.failure: HSA_STATUS_ERROR_OUT_OF_RESOURCES in the next backward after step 1

read: Role-specific compact handles reduce unused MORI symmetric-buffer residency and reach a finite step at lower logged memory than the older full bidirectional fixed-cap handles, but the MBS8 fixed-cap native compact path still runs out of resources after step 1. Keep the patch as a correctness/memory-footprint step, not a throughput promotion. The next fix should reduce remaining backward lifetime or active handle capacity without adding row-path adapters.
